name: Chocolate Crisps
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
2. Grease several baking sheets with butter.
3. In a medium-size bowl, beat the butter and powdered sugar together until creamy.
4. Beat in vanilla extract.
5. Gradually add the egg whites, beating well after each addition.
6. Fold in the sifted flour, then stir in the 2 tablespoons of melted (cooled) butter.
7. Drop small teaspoonfuls of mixture onto prepared baking sheets, spacing them well apart.
8. Flatten each teaspoonful slightly with a fork or your hand.
9. Bake for about 8 minutes, or until lightly browned around the edges.
10. Using a spatula, transfer the cookies to a wire rack and let cool completely.
11. Melt chocolate in a small bowl placed over a pot of hot (but not boiling) water.
12. Once the cookies are cool, spread each one with a thin layer of melted chocolate.
13. Allow the chocolate to set slightly, then sprinkle with finely shredded coconut.
14. Place the coated cookies in a cool place until the chocolate sets completely.
